channel.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
channel.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

image.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
image.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

item.title: Denmark’s Christian Eriksen collapses during World Cup warm-up match
item.description: The football player has a special heart-starting device implanted since he collapsed during a match in 2021.

item.title: Bolivia’s legislature passes law allowing use of troops against protesters
item.description: New law grants president power to use military to clear roadblocks set up amid weeks of antigovernment demonstrations.

item.title: Nigerian forces rescue 360 captives from Boko Haram
item.description: Nigerian forces rescued 360 captives, including women and children from a Boko Haram mountain hideout in Borno State.

item.title: Trump says will not unfreeze Iranian assets before ceasefire deal reached
item.description: Iranian officials have indicated the release of funds would build 'trust' needed to reach lasting deal ending war.

item.title: Police say two suspects have been shot and killed in Israel gun attack
item.description: Police say two suspects have been shot and killed in Israel gun attack.

item.title: Al Jazeera reports from Beirut after Israeli attack
item.description: Multiple explosions have hit Beirut’s southern suburbs as Israel launches new attacks, claiming to target Hezbollah.

item.title: People flood Madrid streets to see Pope Leo’s flower-carpeted procession
item.description: Pope Leo XIV hails Spain’s ‘school of faith’ at Madrid Mass, urges unity amid vast Corpus Domini crowds.

item.title: Authenticity is what American voters want
item.description: Results in US Democratic primary races are showing that old establishment politics no longer work.

item.title: Kimi Antonelli wins delayed Monaco Grand Prix to extend F1 lead
item.description: Antonelli takes his fifth successive Grand Prix win in race interrupted by crashes after asphalt breaks apart.

item.title: Ronaldo, other ageing stars push limits to make history at World Cup 2026
item.description: Experts say sport science is just part of a complex system of factors needed to extend longevity in football.

item.title: From online class to Revolution Square: A Tehran teacher’s routine amid war
item.description: Al Jazeera follows a teacher who navigates a new life marked by a weak internet, soaring prices, and solidarity rallies.

item.title: Iran’s World Cup squad lands in Mexico amid US visa row
item.description: Iranian delegation arrives at their World Cup base camp a day after several members were denied visas by the US.

item.title: Iran’s World Cup team arrives in Mexico as US visa row continues
item.description: Iran’s national football team has arrived in Mexico ahead of its World Cup matches in the US.

item.title: Does Israel really think it can get rid of Hezbollah in Lebanon?
item.description: It’s hard to assume that ‘anything positive’ for Lebanon can come from its talks with Israel, argues analyst Imad Harb.

item.title: The Taiz transplant team looking to begin a medical revolution in Yemen
item.description: Taiz's Cardiac and Vascular Diseases and Kidney Transplant Center provides Yemenis with affordable treatment.

item.title: Peru polls open in Keiko Fujimori, Roberto Sanchez presidential run-off
item.description: Race between right-wing Fujimori and left-wing Sanchez follows first round of voting marred by logistical issues.

item.title: Army frees 360 people abducted by Boko Haram in Nigeria’s Borno state
item.description: Two infants succumbed to the 'harsh conditions' of Boko Haram captivity in the Mandara mountains, says army.

item.title: Israel kills 13 in Gaza as Egypt hosts renewed ‘ceasefire’ talks
item.description: Israeli attacks came near Khan Younis, in Gaza City and Deir el-Balah as Hamas and other groups met mediators in Cairo.

item.title: Fifty-two hours on an Israeli prison ship
item.description: A flotilla activist recounts his detention after Israeli forces intercepted a Gaza-bound solidarity mission at sea.

item.title: Missiles and negotiations: Iran 100 days into the war with US and Israel
item.description: Talks and attacks have continued for months, and could last much longer without a breakthrough.

item.title: Marc Marquez hits 100 career wins with Hungarian MotoGP victory
item.description: The seven-time MotoGP world champion beat KTM's Pedro Acosta to claim his first grand prix victory of the season.

item.title: Israel strikes southern suburbs of Lebanon’s capital Beirut
item.description: Videos show the aftermath of an Israeli attack on Beirut's southern suburbs, with multiple explosions reported.

item.title: Why has the Pentagon raised the risk of Israeli spying to the highest level?
item.description: The US agencies warn against Israeli efforts to collect information on the talks to end the war, according to reports.

item.title: Pope Leo urges youth to be ‘sparks of a new humanity’
item.description: Pope Leo XIV urged young people to be 'sparks of a new humanity'.

item.title: ‘Fear mongering is the main narrative’ in Armenia’s elections
item.description: ‘Fear mongering is the main narrative’ in Armenia’s elections
